transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I'm feeling a little depressed.
When did you first start feeling this way?
Not No. That's a tough 1. Maybe 3 weeks ago.
Can you describe what symptoms of depression you are experiencing?
I'm having loss of appetite.
Are you experiencing any thoughts of harming yourself or feeling hopeless?
No.
Any changes in your sleep patterns, such as trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or sleeping too much?
I am having problems falling asleep and staying asleep.
Any recent stressors or significant life changes that might be contributing to how you're feeling?
Well, I just changed to a different job after we moved.
Have you tried any treatments or medications for your symptoms so far?
No. I know. Then what would I try?
Any history of depression or other mental health conditions in the past?
My father has depression, diagnosed, and he's on treatment. And my mother probably should be on treatment for some intermittent depression.
Have you noticed any changes in your energy levels or concentration recently?
Yeah. My energy has been low, and my concentration is been absolutely horrible. So yeah. Yeah. I think it's safe to say. I'm noticing some issues in those.
Have you experienced any recent changes in weight or noticed any physical symptoms like fatigue or pain?
I definitely feel fatigue. No pain, and my weight doesn't change.
Any history of bipolar disorder or manic episodes in yourself or family?
If anything, it gets higher, so whatever.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
Penicillin.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
No.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No. Because I said I was a man.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you’ve been feeling down for a few weeks with symptoms like low energy, trouble sleeping, and loss of appetite, which may be related to depression. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or run tests, but based on what you told me, medication treatment may be appropriate. A licensed physician will review this note and, if appropriate, may prescribe medication to help. If you notice any worsening symptoms, especially thoughts of self-harm or feeling hopeless, please seek urgent in-person care right away. For now, you can consider supportive steps like gentle routines and self-care. A doctor will also want to check for any medical causes and monitor how you’re doing over time. If you need an in-person exam or testing, please go to urgent care, the emergency department, or see your primary care clinician.
